Overview

Address
36 BROADWAY STREET, COLCHESTER, CT 06415
Phone
8605374606
Certified beds
60
Avg daily residents
55 (91% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1974-06-10
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.26 3.80 3.31 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.94 0.67 0.96 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.43 0.84 0.44 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.89 2.29 1.92 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.37 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.10 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.84
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.62
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.88
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
56.3%
annual
RN turnover
60.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.35
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.98
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-12 7 3 4 44 1 44
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-05-16 35 9 26 389 1 389
Total weighted health score
130.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (51)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
44 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-09
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-09
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-12-03 2025-12-09
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-08-12 2025-09-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-08-12 2025-09-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-08-12 2025-09-12
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-05-06 2025-05-22
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-09-11 2024-09-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-09-11 2024-09-12
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2024-09-11 2024-09-12
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. C 2024-09-11 2024-09-12
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. J 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. J 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2024-08-08 2024-08-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-05-06 2024-06-17
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-05-06 2024-06-17
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-03-25 2024-05-06
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-02-22 2024-05-08
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-02-22 2024-05-08
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-02-22 2024-05-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-22 2024-05-08
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. C 2023-05-16 2023-06-05
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2023-04-03 2023-05-19
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2023-04-03 2023-05-19
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2020-12-21 2021-01-15

Deficiencies 0700 — description Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. 0700.description
Deficiencies 0700 — scope_severity D 0700.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0700 — survey_date 2024-03-25 0700.survey_date
Deficiencies 0700 — tag 0700 0700.tag
Deficiencies 0725 — correction_date 2024-08-08 0725.correction_date
Deficiencies 0725 — description Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. 0725.description
Deficiencies 0725 — scope_severity D 0725.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0725 — survey_date 2024-08-08 0725.survey_date
Deficiencies 0725 — tag 0725 0725.tag
Deficiencies 0730 — correction_date 2024-08-08 0730.correction_date
Deficiencies 0730 — description Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. 0730.description
Deficiencies 0730 — scope_severity D 0730.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0730 — survey_date 2024-08-08 0730.survey_date
Deficiencies 0730 — tag 0730 0730.tag
Deficiencies 0742 — correction_date 2024-09-12 0742.correction_date
Deficiencies 0742 — description Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. 0742.description
Deficiencies 0742 — scope_severity D 0742.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0742 — survey_date 2024-09-11 0742.survey_date
Deficiencies 0742 — tag 0742 0742.tag
Deficiencies 0745 — correction_date 2024-08-08 0745.correction_date
Deficiencies 0745 — correction_date 2021-01-15 0745.correction_date
Deficiencies 0745 — description Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. 0745.description
Deficiencies 0745 — description Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. 0745.description
Deficiencies 0745 — scope_severity D 0745.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0745 — scope_severity D 0745.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0745 — survey_date 2020-12-21 0745.survey_date
Deficiencies 0745 — tag 0745 0745.tag
Deficiencies 0745 — tag 0745 0745.tag
Deficiencies 0757 — correction_date 2021-01-15 0757.correction_date
Deficiencies 0757 — description Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. 0757.description
Deficiencies 0757 — scope_severity D 0757.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0757 — survey_date 2020-12-21 0757.survey_date
Deficiencies 0757 — tag 0757 0757.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2021-01-15 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2020-12-21 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0760 — correction_date 2021-01-15 0760.correction_date
Deficiencies 0760 — description Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. 0760.description
Deficiencies 0760 — scope_severity D 0760.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0760 — survey_date 2020-12-21 0760.survey_date
Deficiencies 0760 — tag 0760 0760.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2025-12-09 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2021-01-15 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description

Deficiencies 0808 — correction_date 2023-06-05 0808.correction_date
Deficiencies 0808 — description Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. 0808.description
Deficiencies 0808 — scope_severity D 0808.scope_severity
